headline: Alberta, Ottawa and oil-sands producers sign MOU to advance the Pathways CCS project — capture target cut to ~16 Mtpa
summary: Alberta and federal governments and the five Oil Sands Alliance members signed an MOU to advance the Pathways CO2 transport-and-storage project (pipeline to a Cold Lake storage hub), with Ottawa extending carbon-capture ITCs to 2035 and Alberta finalising its own incentives; the target was set at ~16 Mtpa by 2035, down ~77% from the original 68 Mt/yr ambition, and tied to support for a proposed ~1 Mbbl/d Alberta-BC oil pipeline.
source: Reuters / BNN Bloomberg / Canada's National Observer
co2crc_note: Global CCS-policy signal: the ITC-backed framework and the 77% target cut sharpen the economics/social-licence debate Australian gas-CCS proponents also face.
extractor_note: Government-industry framework MOU advancing a specific named project; placed in projects.

headline: JAPEX establishes Tomakomai CCS Co. to run Japan's first specified-area offshore storage project
summary: JAPEX announced it established wholly-owned subsidiary Tomakomai CCS Co., Ltd. (TCCS) on 1 July as the implementing entity for the Tomakomai-area offshore CCS project off Hokkaido; JAPEX will seek to transfer its exploratory drilling permit to TCCS, which will then apply to METI for a CO2 storage business permit. Targets CO2 from Idemitsu's Hokkaido Refinery and HEPCO's Tomato-Atsuma power station.
co2crc_note: Japan is a prospective CO2-export partner for Australian storage; advancing Japan's first specified-area offshore CCS grows the APAC storage market CO2CRC operates in.
extractor_note: Corporate/entity-formation milestone for a named project.

headline: PETRONAS details a downhole CO2-logging assessment supporting its CCS agenda (TC19)
summary: Malaysia's PETRONAS published a technical release on a downhole CO2-logging assessment method to strengthen well-integrity and measurement/monitoring for its CCS programme — an MMV/well-surveillance R&D signal from a SE Asian NOC building out multiple Malaysian CCS hubs.
co2crc_note: Downhole CO2-logging/MMV work is directly relevant to CO2CRC's monitoring-and-verification research focus and to SE Asian NOC storage buildout.

headline: Global Carbon Council and CCS+ Initiative sign MoU on high-integrity CCS/CDR methodologies
summary: Qatar-based crediting body the Global Carbon Council signed an MoU with the CCS+ Initiative (announced at the IETA Asia Climate Summit in Hong Kong) to adapt CCS+ methodology materials (DAC, CO2 transport, BECCS) into the GCC programme and assess a dedicated module for industrial point-source capture.
co2crc_note: Crediting-methodology convergence affects the bankability of CCS/CDR credits, including for APAC/Australian projects.
extractor_note: Carbon-market methodology MoU; placed in markets.

headline: Verra opens consultation on a major revision to its CO2-in-concrete methodology (VM0043 v2.0)
summary: Verra launched a public consultation (10 Jul-10 Aug) on revising VM0043, the VCS methodology for using waste CO2 as a feedstock in concrete production, aligning it with Verra's DAC (VMD0056) and CCS (VM0049) methodologies and splitting removal vs reduction labels; sponsored by CarbonCure Technologies.
co2crc_note: CO2-utilisation crediting rulebook development; peripheral to CO2CRC's storage focus but relevant to CCU credit markets.
extractor_note: Radar item — consultation opened 10 Jul, just outside the 13-14 Jul window.
